old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-u7d","title":"Implement Google OAuth 2.0 backend","description":"Implement direct Google OAuth 2.0 / OIDC flow in Go using golang.org/x/oauth2. Endpoints: GET /api/auth/login (redirect to Google), GET /api/auth/callback (exchange code, upsert user record, set signed HTTP-only session cookie), POST /api/auth/logout (clear cookie). Any Google account can sign up (open). Store user in SQLite users table on first login.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-tam","title":"Investigate and define testing strategy for the project","description":"The initial unit/integration test approach was closed without merging. Need to decide on the right testing pattern before writing any tests.

Consider:
- E2E tests: test the full stack (browser to backend)
- Component testing: test individual frontend components in isolation
- Contract testing: verify API contracts between frontend and backend
- Unit tests: keep only for genuinely complex functions (business logic, parsing)

Deliverables: decision doc with chosen strategy and rationale, example test(s) demonstrating the pattern, and guidelines on when unit tests are appropriate vs the chosen higher-level approach.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T19:35:04Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-15T19:35:04Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-clu","title":"Add validate tags and validator calls to UpdateTaskRequest and AddSymbolRequest","description":"M1+M2: Two handler DTOs are missing go-playground/validator struct tags and validator.Struct() calls, inconsistent with CreateTaskRequest which was correctly updated.

1. dto.go UpdateTaskRequest: add validate tags:
   Text *string validate:"omitempty,min=1,max=1000"
   Priority *string validate:"omitempty,oneof=high medium low"
   Then call taskValidator.Struct(&req) in the Update handler after decode, return 400 on failure.

2. stocks_dto.go AddSymbolRequest: add validate tags:
   Symbol string validate:"required,min=1,max=20"
   Then call validator.Struct(&body) in AddSymbol handler after decode (reuse the existing taskValidator or create a shared one), return 400 on failure.

Both fixes are in the same handler package and can ship in a single PR.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:23Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:23Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-9ya","title":"Service layer hardening: url.QueryEscape, io.LimitReader, errors.Is","description":"M3+M4+M5: Three small hardening fixes across the service and repository layers, all shippable in one PR.

M3 (service/stocks.go:193): Symbol not URL-encoded in Finnhub quote URL. Change:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", symbol)
to:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", url.QueryEscape(symbol))
SearchSymbols already does this correctly — apply the same pattern to fetchFinnhub.

M4 (service/news.go:92): io.ReadAll on upstream response body with no size limit. Change to:
  io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 1<<20)) — consistent with stocks service.

M5 (repository/sqlite_task_repository.go:95): sql.ErrNoRows compared with == instead of errors.Is. Change to errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) for future-proofing against wrapped errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:28Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:28Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-9cq","title":"Infrastructure and config hardening","description":"LOW/infrastructure items that can ship together:

1. CORS origin hardcoded to localhost:5173 (middleware/cors.go:10). Make configurable via CORS_ORIGIN env var, defaulting to http://localhost:5173. Update config.go to read it and wire into cors middleware. Update .env.example and README env vars table.

2. Add govulncheck to CI/CD pipeline (if one exists) or document as a manual step in CLAUDE.md/README. Run: go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest && govulncheck ./...

3. Dashboard goroutine concurrent struct writes (dashboard.go:57-127): Add a comment above var resp documenting the safety invariant — each goroutine writes to a distinct field, g.Wait() provides the happens-before guarantee for the final read. Low-risk but documents the assumption for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:41Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:41Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-frq","title":"Harden Go backend HTTP layer","description":"Create shared internal packages to eliminate boilerplate and inconsistency across handlers and services:
1. internal/response/ package — WriteJSON(w, status, v) and WriteError(w, status, msg) helpers to replace repeated json.NewEncoder patterns and silent encode-error discards across all handlers
2. internal/httpclient/ or service-level helper — shared pattern for external API calls (set headers, io.LimitReader body, unmarshal JSON) currently duplicated across weather, news, stocks, commute, sunrise, quotes services
3. Centralize sentinel errors — ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidInput etc. in a shared package so handlers can use errors.Is() consistently
4. Shared validator instance — single go-playground/validator/v10 instance in internal/validate/ or similar, reused across all handlers
Goal: reduce per-handler boilerplate, ensure consistent HTTP status codes and response format across all endpoints.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:00:33Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:00:33Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-lmc","title":"Fix label picker: clicks not registering on dropdown items","description":"Label picker dropdown opens correctly but clicking label items to assign them does nothing. Root causes: (1) mousedown outside-click handler may close picker during concurrent re-renders since pickerRef.current.contains() could fail; (2) no optimistic update on assignLabel means zero immediate visual feedback. Fix: add onMouseDown stopPropagation on dropdown div, add zIndex to open task row, add optimistic update to assignLabel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:58:00Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:58:00Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-0gm","title":"Fix AssignLabel SQL: ON CONFLICT clause incompatible with partial unique index","description":"AssignLabel repository method uses 'ON CONFLICT(task_id, label_id) DO UPDATE' but the unique constraint on task_label_assignments is a partial unique index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) created separately, not an inline UNIQUE constraint in CREATE TABLE. SQLite's ON CONFLICT (columns) clause only recognizes PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraints defined in the table definition, not standalone CREATE UNIQUE INDEX. Result: every POST /api/tasks/{id}/labels returns 500. Fix: replace ON CONFLICT upsert with plain INSERT — service layer already guards against duplicates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T04:06:36Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-20T04:06:36Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-75c","title":"Implement EncryptionService for calendar_ics_url in user_settings","description":"HIGH security finding from PR #29 review. The calendar_ics_url column stores ICS feed URLs which frequently contain long-lived auth tokens (Google Calendar private feed URLs, Outlook tokens). These are effectively bearer credentials and must NOT be stored in plaintext. Implement an EncryptionService in backend/internal/service/encryption.go with: Encrypt(plaintext string) (ciphertext string, error) and Decrypt(ciphertext string) (plaintext string, error) using AES-GCM with a key derived from a new ENCRYPTION_KEY env var. Inject into the user settings service. Encrypt on write, decrypt on read. Update .env.example and README with the new env var.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:37:09Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:37:09Z"}

new_value: PR #37 merged — AES-256-GCM encryption for sensitive fields with required ENCRYPTION_KEY

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-gsd","title":"Add SSRF scheme validation for calendar_ics_url on backend","description":"The backend fetches calendar_ics_url without validating the scheme. A user (or attacker with access) could supply file://, ftp://, or internal network URLs (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/). Add validation in the user settings service or calendar service: only allow https:// (and optionally http://) schemes before fetching. Return a 422 if the scheme is disallowed. File: backend/internal/service/user_settings.go or calendar.go. Discovered during security review of PR #33 (bd-d1h).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:30Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-18T03:07:30Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-gdi","title":"Add startup validation: reject CORS_ORIGIN=* when Credentials: true","description":"PR #26 review MEDIUM (security): cors middleware always emits Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. If CORS_ORIGIN is set to * the browser rejects it, but the server still emits the unsafe header pair — a misconfiguration footgun. Add startup validation in config.Load() or main.go that errors/panics if CORSOrigin is '*' or empty after trimming, consistent with how SESSION_SECRET is validated at startup. File: backend/internal/middleware/cors.go:14, backend/internal/config/config.go. Discovered during infra-hardening-9cq PR review.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:22:06Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-17T00:22:06Z"}
